summary: Mobile responsiveness fix verification COMPLETE. All three targeted issues from iteration_49 are now FIXED: 1) Stats grid displays correctly as 2x2 (144px 144px on Android, 159px 159px on iPhone), 2) Sovereign badge is properly positioned at bottom-right with auto width (x=56-86, width=292px vs previous x=-12, width=360-390px), 3) Scroll functionality works correctly. Applied fix to mobile-enhancements.css to exclude fixed elements from max-width constraint.

fixes_applied.change: Modified line 241-243 from '* { max-width: 100vw !important; }' to '*:not([class*="fixed"]):not([data-testid="sovereign-badge"]) { max-width: 100vw !important; }'
Added specific CSS rule for sovereign badge with 'width: auto !important' and 'max-width: fit-content !important' to override all * rules

context_for_next_testing_agent: Mobile responsiveness issues from iteration_49 are all resolved. Stats grid 2x2, sovereign badge positioning, and scroll all work correctly on Android (360x800) and iPhone (390x844) viewports. The root cause of the badge issue was a blanket * { max-width: 100vw !important } rule in mobile-enhancements.css that was overriding all other CSS exclusions.

stats_grid_2x2.original_problem: Stats grid showing single column instead of 2x2 on mobile
stats_grid_2x2.root_cause: CSS rule in index.css that forced grid-cols-2 to 1fr
stats_grid_2x2.fix_applied_by_main_agent: Removed grid-cols-2 from the 1fr override rule

sovereign_badge.original_problem: Badge taking full viewport width (x=-12, width=360/390px)
sovereign_badge.root_cause: Multiple CSS files had * { max-width: 100% !important } rules, but mobile-enhancements.css line 241 had '* { max-width: 100vw !important }' that overrode all exclusions
sovereign_badge.additional_fix_applied: 1) Added exclusion for fixed elements and badge in mobile-enhancements.css, 2) Added specific rule in index.css for badge with width: auto and max-width: fit-content
sovereign_badge.verified: FIXED - Badge now shows x=56-86, width=292px
